concept: Server Components vs Client Components (The Hydration Bridge)

executionTimeline.description: User requests the page. The Next.js server identifies that the root is a Server Component.
The server executes the async function, fetches data from the DB, and renders the component to a special format (RSC payload).
The server sends the static HTML and the RSC payload (which contains the logic for Client Components) to the browser.
The browser displays the static HTML immediately. Then, the React runtime 'hydrates' the Client Component, attaching event listeners to the existing HTML.
The user clicks the button. The Client Component's state changes, triggering a local re-render in the browser without contacting the server.

theFix: To avoid 'Text content did not match' hydration errors, ensure that you don't use browser-only values (like window.innerWidth) during the initial render. Use a 'useEffect' to update those values after the component has mounted on the client.
